notes: Silver DQ rules for base.anthropic_observed_exposure. Aggregation: Bronze (task, SOC) rows filtered (drop task_name='none') then grouped by soc_code. observed_exposure_pct = sum(task_pct_split); automation_pct/augmentation_pct are volume-weighted means in 0-100 percent units (consistent with observed_exposure_pct and every other _pct field in the project). Expected row count: 588 SOCs (EDA). Row-count band 500-650 accommodates minor release drift. SOC coverage threshold revised from spec's 80% to 60% per EDA finding (61.3% actual, dataset limitation not ingestor defect). Sum-invariant threshold 98.2 +/- 0.5 reflects 100 - 1.78 (dropped 'none' placeholder). SLV-AOE-009 and SLV-AOE-010 were removed 2026-04-17 because they referenced a `filtered_pct` column that does not exist in the Silver schema. The Silver schema (per spec and as-built) is: record_id, soc_code, soc_title, observed_exposure_pct, automation_pct, augmentation_pct, task_count, soc_match, source_release, promoted_at. Adding filtered_pct would be a spec change and is out of scope. See governance/audit-trail/2026-04-17-dq-rule-writer-anthropic-economic-index-post-execution.md for rationale.

rules.name: Grain uniqueness: soc_code
rules.sql: SELECT soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.anthropic_observed_exposure GROUP BY soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: Silver grain is one row per soc_code. Duplicate SOC codes would cause fan-out in Gold JOINs and double-count observed exposure. The Silver transformer aggregates Bronze (task, SOC) rows to one row per SOC, so any duplicate indicates a groupby or promote bug.
rules.rationale: EDA: '588 SOCs after fan-out and aggregation.' Spec: 'Grain: One row per SOC code. Dedup grain: [soc_code].' Threshold: 0 duplicates across ~588 rows.

rules.name: observed_exposure_pct range: 0 <= x <= 100
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.anthropic_observed_exposure WHERE observed_exposure_pct IS NULL OR observed_exposure_pct < 0 OR observed_exposure_pct > 100
rules.description: observed_exposure_pct is the SOC-level sum of split task shares, in percent units (0-100). It must be non-null and within [0, 100]. Values > 100 indicate the fan-out split was skipped (double-counting); values < 0 indicate a sign error.
rules.rationale: EDA aggregation strategy: 'Sum-based aggregation produces values ranging 0.0015 - 7.51 across 588 SOCs.' Spec: 'observed_exposure_pct range 0 <= x <= 100.' Per EDA max is ~7.51, well inside the [0, 100] band; a violation would signal a fan-out bug. Threshold: 0 out-of-range or null values.

rules.name: Global share invariant: SUM(observed_exposure_pct) approximately 98.2
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN ABS(SUM(observed_exposure_pct) - 98.22) > 0.5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM base.anthropic_observed_exposure
rules.description: After dropping the task_name='none' placeholder (1.78% of global share) and even-splitting multi-SOC tasks, SUM(observed_exposure_pct) across all Silver rows must equal 98.22 within +/- 0.5. A larger deviation indicates either (a) the 'none' placeholder leaked into Silver, (b) the fan-out split was skipped, or (c) SOC coverage against the task->SOC bridge dropped.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'sum(observed_exposure_pct) = 98.22, matching sum(pct) after removing none.' Spec: 'SUM(observed_exposure_pct) ~= 98.2 +/- 0.5.' Tolerance +/- 0.5 chosen to accommodate float rounding + any tasks lost to SOC-bridge filtering, while still catching a failed 'none' drop (1.78 delta would push out of band).

rules.name: Row count within expected range: 500-650
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 500 OR cnt > 650 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.anthropic_observed_exposure)
rules.description: Silver row count must be 500-650 SOCs. 588 observed in EDA against release_2025_03_27; band is ~+/- 10% to allow modest release drift. Count outside this range indicates SOC normalization collapsed or exploded, the Bronze row count shifted beyond its band, or the 'none' filter was misapplied.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Unique SOC codes derivable from Anthropic data (via O*NET): 588.' Spec originally said 700-900 based on an optimistic estimate; EDA revealed actual is 588. Revised band 500-650 (centered at 588 with ~+/- 10% headroom) -- differs from spec's 700-900 because spec was pre-EDA.
rules.threshold_divergence_note: Diverged from spec's '700-900' based on EDA: 588 SOCs actually derivable from Anthropic source. Revised to 500-650 (centered on observed 588).

rules.name: task_count >= 1: 100%
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.anthropic_observed_exposure WHERE task_count IS NULL OR task_count < 1
rules.description: Every SOC row must have task_count >= 1 (the aggregate groupby produces rows only when at least one task contributes). A 0 or NULL task_count means the grouping logic is broken.
rules.rationale: EDA per-SOC task volume: 'min: 1, median: 4, mean: 6.94, max: 141.' Spec: 'task_count >= 1 100%.' Min is guaranteed 1 by construction of the groupby. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: SOC match coverage: >= 60% of rows
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN match_rate < 0.60 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN soc_match THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS match_rate FROM base.anthropic_observed_exposure)
rules.description: At least 60% of Silver rows must have soc_match = true (SOC exists in consumable.occupation_profiles). Revised from spec's 80% per EDA finding (61.3% actual). Below 60% indicates either the source SOC set shifted dramatically, occupation_profiles was rebuilt with a different SOC universe, or the match logic is broken.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'SOC coverage against consumable.occupation_profiles: 61.3% (510 / 832).' EDA recommendation: 'Revise P0 threshold from >=80% to >=60%.' Domain context: 'Revise P0 threshold from >=80% to >=60% SOC coverage. Document the dataset limitation in the contract. This is not an ingestor defect.' Threshold: match_rate >= 0.60.
rules.threshold_divergence_note: Diverged from spec's '>=80%' per EDA finding that actual coverage is 61.3% (510/832). Claude traffic skews toward knowledge work; 315 target SOCs in trades/production/transport/food-service have zero Anthropic tasks mapped. Documented in domain context as dataset limitation, not ingestor defect.

rules.name: soc_code format: XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.anthropic_observed_exposure WHERE soc_code IS NULL OR NOT regexp_matches(soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}$')
rules.description: Silver soc_code must be normalized XX-XXXX. Unlike Bronze (which carries exactly one NULL soc_code for the 'none' placeholder), Silver must never have a NULL SOC because the 'none' row is filtered pre-aggregation.
rules.rationale: Spec Silver transformations: 'SOC normalization: Standardize to XX-XXXX format.' EDA: 'Silver transformer MUST filter task_name=none before join' -- so no NULL SOC in Silver. Consistent with SLV-KAI-002 pattern. Threshold: 0 null/malformed codes.

rules.name: record_id uniqueness and not null
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.anthropic_observed_exposure WHERE record_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY record_id HAVING cnt > 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'NULL_CHECK' AS record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.anthropic_observed_exposure WHERE record_id IS NULL HAVING cnt > 0
rules.description: Surrogate key record_id (computed via compute_grain_id with prefix 'aoe') must be non-null and unique. A duplicate or null indicates a grain-hash collision or derivation bug in promote_silver.
rules.rationale: Spec: 'Promote pattern: compute_grain_id(row, [soc_code], prefix=aoe).' Deterministic hash of natural key should produce 0 collisions for ~588 rows. Consistent with GLD-AIE-002 and SLV-KAI record_id patterns. Threshold: 0 dup/null.

rules.name: automation_pct / augmentation_pct range: 0 <= x <= 100
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.anthropic_observed_exposure WHERE (automation_pct IS NOT NULL AND (automation_pct < 0 OR automation_pct > 100)) OR (augmentation_pct IS NOT NULL AND (augmentation_pct < 0 OR augmentation_pct > 100))
rules.description: automation_pct and augmentation_pct are emitted by the ingestor in 0-100 percent units, consistent with observed_exposure_pct and every other _pct field in this project. Values outside [0, 100] indicate a unit-conversion bug or a corrupted aggregation. Revised 2026-04-17 after DQ execution revealed an original 0-1 fraction-range threshold was wrong for the as-built schema (500 FAILs against rows like 11-1011 Chief Executives automation_pct=24.31, augmentation_pct=65.81, which are correct 0-100 values).
rules.rationale: As-built ingestor: automation_pct = (directive + feedback_loop) * 100, augmentation_pct = (task_iteration + validation + learning) * 100 (spec implementation notes 1-3). Source fractions are 0-1; ingestor scales by 100, producing 0-100. Bronze EDA confirms rows like 38.30/36.17 for observed data. Threshold: 0 out-of-range values.
rules.revision_note: Unit contract fix: threshold was 0-1 fraction, revised to 0-100 percent to match as-built ingestor output and Bronze/Silver/Gold _pct convention.

rules.name: source_release pinned to release_2025_03_27
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.anthropic_observed_exposure WHERE source_release IS NULL OR source_release <> 'release_2025_03_27'
rules.description: source_release is pinned to 'release_2025_03_27' per the release-selection logic (newer releases lack task-level aggregates). A different value indicates the ingestor auto-followed HEAD or picked a release that shouldn't have task_pct_v2.csv -- breaking all downstream assumptions.
rules.rationale: Domain context: 'Hard-pin release_2025_03_27 in the ingestor. Do not auto-follow HEAD.' EDA note: 'Newer releases (release_2026_01_15, release_2026_03_24) contain only raw conversation snapshots.' When a future release does re-publish task-level aggregates, this rule will be updated to accept the new release id. Threshold: 0 mismatches.

rules.name: soc_title non-null: 100%
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.anthropic_observed_exposure WHERE soc_title IS NULL OR TRIM(soc_title) = ''
rules.description: soc_title is required per Silver schema. A null or empty title means the SOC normalization lookup failed.
rules.rationale: Spec Silver schema: soc_title required=yes. Threshold: 0 null/empty titles.

rules.name: Outlier monitoring: observed_exposure_pct > 5 flagged for review
rules.sql: SELECT soc_code, soc_title, observed_exposure_pct FROM base.anthropic_observed_exposure WHERE observed_exposure_pct > 5 ORDER BY observed_exposure_pct DESC
rules.description: SOCs with observed_exposure_pct > 5 represent concentrated Claude adoption (> 5% of all classified traffic on a single occupation). Expected count is small (EDA: max 7.51; top-1 SOC 15-1199 at ~5-8%). More than 10 such SOCs indicates either the 'none' placeholder leaked into Silver and inflated a single SOC, or a release-over-release shift in Claude's user distribution.
rules.rationale: EDA: 'Sum-based aggregation produces values ranging 0.0015 - 7.51 across 588 SOCs. Top 10 SOCs account for 35.7% of total exposure; top 50 account for 71.4%.' Edge Case #8: 'Catch-all SOC 15-1199 receives 141 Anthropic tasks ... top-1 SOC observed_exposure_pct should be 5-10%.' Threshold: result_count <= 10 -- monitoring, not blocking.

rules.name: Low-confidence SOCs (task_count == 1) tracked
rules.sql: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN task_count = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS low_conf_rate FROM base.anthropic_observed_exposure
rules.description: Informational: proportion of Silver rows backed by only one task. Expected ~33% per EDA. Tracked for drift monitoring; no threshold enforced.
rules.rationale: EDA Edge Case #9: 'Very-low-coverage SOCs (task_count == 1): ~195 (33% of source SOCs). P2 rule: flag any Silver row with task_count == 1 as low-confidence. Consider a confidence_tier field.' Demoted to P3 because this is a structural property of the data, not a defect. Threshold: tracked (informational).
